must_have_breakdown.skill: 8+ years of experience in Car Rental / Fleet Management / Mobility industry
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Candidate has ~9 years in Facilities and Transport Management, but focused on employee/corporate cab operations (IT company transport), not car rental or fleet management as a business.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Strong experience in Operations Management + Sales / Business Development
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Operations management is evident across all roles, but zero evidence of Sales or Business Development activity anywhere in the resume.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Proven track record in P&L ownership and profitability management
Experience handling corporate clients / B2B accounts
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Managed corporate transport bookings and vendor billing at Ventra Health and EnviriIndia, but as an internal ops function, not as a revenue-generating B2B account manager.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Strong leadership and team management experience
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Holds 'Team Lead' title at Ventra Health with manpower management and team leadership listed, but no team size or direct reportee count mentioned.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Good commercial acumen and negotiation skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced target-driven environment
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Managed 24/7 transport for 4000 employees at Cognizant, indicating operational pressure handling, but no evidence of target-driven sales or revenue metrics.

reasoning: Reject — wrong role level and scope. This candidate is an operations executor, not a Profit Centre Head. The JD demands P&L ownership, B2B sales, and branch profitability accountability — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. The candidate's 9 years is entirely in corporate employee transport operations (IT sector cab management), not in car rental or mobility-as-a-service, which the recruiter has explicitly flagged as a must-have domain. There is no evidence of revenue generation, sales pipeline ownership, or P&L management — the three most critical requirements for a Branch Head role. The highest-signal strength here is operational process management (MIS, vendor coordination, fleet compliance), but that maps to a Senior Transport Executive, not a Profit Centre Head. Unless the hiring pool is completely dry, pass on this candidate.
strengths: FLEET & TRANSPORT OPERATIONS DEPTH: Nine years of end-to-end transport operations across Cognizant (4000-employee fleet, 24/7), RouteMatic, and EnviriIndia — covering vendor management, GPS tracking, compliance, EHS audits, and full MIS ownership, which gives a solid operational foundation.

COMPLIANCE AND SAFETY MANAGEMENT: Consistent evidence of driver training, vehicle safety checks, EHS audits, and female employee safety protocols across multiple employers, indicating disciplined process ownership in a regulated transport environment.

FACILITIES + TRANSPORT DUAL CAPABILITY: Manages both FM and transport functions simultaneously at Ventra Health and EnviriIndia, including budgeting, vendor contracts, and petty cash — showing broader operational accountability beyond pure transport.
gaps: NO P&L OR REVENUE OWNERSHIP: The Profit Centre Head role requires full branch P&L accountability and revenue growth targets — this candidate has never owned a P&L, driven B2B sales, or been accountable for branch profitability in any capacity. This is the single most disqualifying gap.

WRONG INDUSTRY SEGMENT: Recruiter explicitly wants passenger management from car rental/fleet mobility (not goods, Rapido, or Uber) — this candidate's entire career is in IT-company internal employee transport, which is a cost-centre support function, not a commercial mobility business. Domain fit is fundamentally misaligned.

scoring_factors.reason: Latest role ended March 2026 and candidate appears currently between roles, so no notice period penalty applies.
No location stated on resume, but Cognizant and other employers suggest Hyderabad presence — cannot confirm mismatch, so no penalty applied.
Candidate has 9 years total experience, well above the 4-year minimum threshold (half of 4 relevant years required).
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD.

availability_reason: Latest role at Ventra Health ended March 2026 — candidate is currently between roles.
self_reported_unverified: Team leadership claim at Ventra Health — no team size, direct reports count, or org structure provided to validate scope of leadership.
Budgeting and cost control listed as a responsibility — no budget size, savings achieved, or financial outcomes quantified anywhere.
End-to-end MIS ownership claimed repeatedly — no examples of dashboards built, reporting tools used, or business decisions influenced by MIS outputs.
interview_flags: Ask what the actual team size was under direct management at Ventra Health and what decisions the candidate owned vs. escalated.
Probe whether any experience involved commercial car rental, leasing, or mobility-as-a-service clients — as opposed to internal IT company transport.
Ask directly: has the candidate ever owned a revenue target, a sales quota, or a P&L line? What was the result?
Clarify the gap and role overlap: Ventra Health is listed as Aug 2024 to March 2026 — verify if March 2026 is accurate or a typo for 2025.
Ask what 'budgeting and cost control' meant in practice — what was the budget size, who approved it, and what cost reductions were achieved?
